strengths: 18+ years of full-stack experience with deep TypeScript/Node.js work at multiple companies (re:cinq, Optilyz, RIDE)
Direct AI-agent development experience: MCP code generators and agentic-workflow frameworks at current role
Strong engineering culture fit: TDD, pairing, CI/CD, trunk-based development, testing workshops, and open-source test tooling authorship
concerns: No explicit Nest.js experience; frontend history leans heavily on Ember.js with only one clearly React-based role
No Kotlin and only dated Java exposure (robotics work 2016-2018), a listed secondary stack
Notable time invested in DLang niche ecosystem, which has limited direct transfer to the primary stack

key_factors.factor: Gaps in Nest.js, React depth, and Kotlin

response.justification: The candidate comfortably exceeds the 6+ years requirement with strong TypeScript/Node.js product experience, cloud (AWS, Kubernetes, Docker), MongoDB/SQL, and a rare, directly relevant AI-agent development background that matches the internal-tooling mission. Testing expertise and craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD) are demonstrably strong. Gaps are framework-specific (Nest.js, Vitest, modern React depth, Kotlin) rather than conceptual, and this profile of engineer typically picks those up quickly.
